text.title: Laravel Enterprise Patterns Failure Modes
text.summary: 5 antipatterns observed in real Laravel 10/11 codebases. Each pairs symptom + root cause + concrete fix.

antipattern.symptom: Attacker self-promotes by POSTing `is_admin=1`; row is updated; audit log shows nothing unusual.
antipattern.fix: Switch to `$request->validated()`; declare explicit FormRequest rules; add a feature test asserting protected fields are dropped from input.

antipattern.symptom: List endpoint executes one SELECT per row; query log shows `SELECT * FROM organizations WHERE id = ?` repeated 200 times.
antipattern.root-cause: Iteration over a collection touched a lazy relation without eager-loading.
antipattern.fix: Eager-load via `Model::with('organization')->get()`. Enable `Model::preventLazyLoading(! app()->isProduction())` in `AppServiceProvider` to fail loudly in dev.

antipattern.symptom: User receives a "Welcome" email; backend rolled the user creation back due to a downstream failure; user is now missing.
antipattern.root-cause: `Mail::to(...)->send(...)` ran inside the `DB::transaction` block before commit.
antipattern.fix: Move dispatch to `DB::afterCommit(fn() => ...)` or use `Mail::to(...)->queue(...)` so the email is enqueued only on commit.

antipattern.symptom: Unit test cannot replace the real `PaymentGateway` with a fake; the SUT calls `app(PaymentGateway::class)` directly.
antipattern.root-cause: Author resolved the service via `app(...)` instead of receiving it in the constructor.
antipattern.fix: Switch to constructor injection; have the container autowire. Reserve `app(...)` for binding setup and rare runtime overrides.

antipattern.symptom: Queue worker retries on transient failure; customer is double-charged.
antipattern.root-cause: Job does not implement `ShouldBeUnique`; retries dispatch the same work.
antipattern.fix: Implement `ShouldBeUnique`; define `uniqueId()` (e.g. invoice id + state machine version); add a feature test that retries twice and verifies one external charge.
